title: Test suite: Replica backup/restore
author: jesperpedersen
body:
We need to create a full test suite that will test

* Test case: Full backup from a replica (PostgreSQL 17+)
* Test case: Incremental backup from a replica (PostgreSQL 17+)
* Test case: Full backup from a replica (PostgreSQL 14)
* Test case: Incremental backup from a replica (PostgreSQL 14)

So, we need to configure a primary/replica setup, and make sure that backups can be taken from the replica instance instead of the primary one.

Then we need to do the same for a restore based on these backups

title: Parallel Azure blob upload using worker pool
author: shashidhar-bm
body:
With #1212, we introduced parallelism across different storage engines. However, file uploads within the Azure storage engine are still performed one at a time. 
This issue brings worker-pool-based parallel file upload to Azure (same as how s3 upload works today).

title: feat: parallel fan-out for remote storage engines
author: shashidhar-bm
body:
Add a unified remote upload node that runs S3, Azure, and SSH uploads in parallel threads
also Add MCTF integration tests that bring up all three backends together and prove the uploads actually run in parallel

title: Wrong value of "Path" key in File object of manifest file for incremental backup workflow v14-16
author: ashu3103
body:
**Describe the bug**

As per the docs the "Path" key of the File object in manifest file should be: The associated string value is the path of the file relative to the root of the backup directory. But currently only file name is displayed rather than the relative path.

Expected:

```
base/16615/INCREMENTAL.6117,637bed75f4fe601a2f99eb1c7508a782199cb2199e6179bf40b3b572cc84cb72e6ec82a4ee62ed70630ec4546f2c780744039543b218a057f85b8d601137ab9f
```

Actual:

```
INCREMENTAL.6117,637bed75f4fe601a2f99eb1c7508a782199cb2199e6179bf40b3b572cc84cb72e6ec82a4ee62ed70630ec4546f2c780744039543b218a057f85b8d601137ab9f
```

title: check.sh crashes on port conflict
author: shashidhar-bm
body:
`check.sh` currently fails if the default test port is already in use, requiring manual intervention to choose a new port.
Also , when setup aborts early, `cleanup()` attempts to use a configuration file that was never created, resulting in a misleading secondary error during shutdown.

title: Backup worflow for tablespaces (v14) gives segfault
author: ashu3103
body:
**Describe the bug**

On executing the full backup command with Tablespaces (no Tablespaces no bug) the server gives segmentation fault. The error originates from `pgmoneta_receive_archive_files` function in `archive.c` which I think is not well tested for lower PostgreSQL versions with Tablespaces. (@Jubilee101 @jesperpedersen can you confirm this)

```
SUMMARY: UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer: undefined-behavior /home/ashu3103/Desktop/pgmoneta/src/libpgmoneta/archive.c:362:19
```

title: Backup setup fails non-deterministically in CI
author: shashidhar-bm
body:
I've observed a recurring issue in CI where some tests fail intermittently without any apparent reason. Interestingly, re-running the same job often results in a successful run without any changes.

The failure that keeps surfacing is:
```
Failed tests:
  - test_cli_archive (test_cli.c:234) - backup failed during setup - check server is online and backup configuration
```

Need to find a way to improve CI consistency.

title: SCRAM messages split across network segments can underflow the length-prefixed parsers
author: fheikens
body:
A SCRAM authentication message (`AuthenticationSASLContinue` / `SASLFinal`) that arrives split across multiple TCP segments is parsed after the first `read()` returns. The parser then computes `sasl_continue->length - 9` (and similar offsets) on a partial message; because `length` is unsigned, a short message underflows to a huge value that reaches `get_scram_attribute()` / `base64_decode()`, risking a crash. The same single-read assumption exists in every SCRAM exchange (server-side and client-side).

This is the counterpart of pgagroal#834.

### Fix

Add a `read_complete_message()` that keeps reading until the byte count declared in the message header has arrived and rejects a malformed declared length; use it for the SCRAM exchanges; guard every SCRAM parse site against short messages and missing attributes so a malformed message fails authentication cleanly instead of crashing.

### Acceptance

New MCTF regression tests covering single-segment, split-segment, truncated-by-close, and invalid-declared-length cases.

title: Test: Not double compressed with server-side compression
author: AhmedMabrouk22
body:
When using `compression = server-zstd` in `pgmoneta.conf`  with `wal_compression = zstd` in `postgresql.conf`, pgmoneta should not apply a second layer of compression on top of what PostgreSQL already did.
Related to #944

title: Local backup isn't retained when local is configured alongside another storage engine
author: shashidhar-bm
body: For example, with `storage_engine = local, s3`, the backup should be retained both locally and in S3. Currently, however, the local backup data is deleted after it is uploaded to S3.

title: Failed backup leaves junk in the cloud
author: shashidhar-bm
body: the failure cleanup only deletes the local side.,,nd it doesn't remove what was already uploaded to the remote backends.

title: Restore fails when config files live outside the data directory
author: shashidhar-bm
body:
Today I was using a PostgreSQL cluster installed the normal Debian way (`apt install postgresql`). 
The backup ran fine, but the restore failed, 

When I checked the log, it said missing `postgresql.conf`, 
```
ERROR vfile_local.c:66 vfile_local: Failed to open file '/tmp/pgm-s3-test/backup/primary/backup/20260614221817/data//postgresql.conf.zstd' (mode='r'): No such file or directory
ERROR extraction.c:162 extraction: failed to create reader for /tmp/pgm-s3-test/backup/primary/backup/20260614221817/data//postgresql.conf.zstd
```

Digging further, I noticed that on Debian/Ubuntu, PostgreSQL keeps its configuration files under `/etc/postgresql/...` outside the data directory. As a result, `postgresql.conf` and `pg_hba.conf` were never included in the backup, and the restore's "excluded files" step aborts when those files are missing.

What I don't understand is why this hasn't come up before. Could this be something specific to my machine?

title: Remove s3 delete subcommand
author: Amr-Shams
body:
`pgmoneta-cli s3 delete <server> <prefix>` leaves orphaned backup.info on disk. After that:

1. pgmoneta-cli list shows a phantom backup
2. Verification reads broken metadata and errors out
3. No command reconciles the state

pgmoneta-cli delete handles both local+S3 atomically.

title: pgmoneta_connect: use bounded pgmoneta_snprintf for port formatting
author: fheikens
body:
In `pgmoneta_connect` (`src/libpgmoneta/network.c`) the backend port is formatted into the stack buffer `sport` with an unbounded `sprintf`:

```c
char sport[6];
...
sprintf(&sport[0], "%d", port);
```

The buffer is already correctly sized — six bytes holds the maximum port (65535) plus its NUL — so this is not an overflow. It is a consistency/hardening gap: every other formatted write in this file already uses the bounded `pgmoneta_snprintf` (e.g. lines 171, 185, 220), and the sister project pgexporter already uses the bounded form here.

Proposal: replace the `sprintf` with `pgmoneta_snprintf(&sport[0], sizeof(sport), "%d", port)` to match the surrounding code. No behavioural change for valid ports.

Context: surfaced while cross-checking the equivalent pgagroal fix (pgagroal#926 / pgagroal#927) across the sister projects.
